nodes.title: Start total at zero
nodes.why: A numeric starting point makes each valid number easy to accumulate.
nodes.alternative: Use the first valid number as initial value.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple and safe, with no special-case logic.

nodes.title: Only add valid numbers
nodes.why: This skips strings, objects, and `NaN`, so the sum stays reliable.
nodes.alternative: Coerce values with `Number(el)` and sum everything that converts.
nodes.tradeoff: More strict and predictable, but intentionally ignores numeric strings like `'3'`.

nodes.title: Return the accumulated result
nodes.why: After one full pass, `total` already contains the final answer.
nodes.tradeoff: Very readable flow, with a few more lines than functional one-liners.

nodes.title: Filter invalid values first
nodes.why: Filtering upfront keeps the reduce step focused on pure addition.
nodes.alternative: Use only `reduce` and check inside the reducer callback.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleaner reducer logic, but creates an intermediate filtered array.

nodes.title: Reduce numbers to one total
nodes.why: `reduce` combines all remaining numbers into one accumulated value.
nodes.alternative: Loop with `for...of` and mutate a running total.
nodes.tradeoff: Compact functional style, but can feel denser for beginners.

nodes.title: Seed reduce with zero
nodes.why: The seed guarantees a correct result even when no valid numbers remain.
nodes.alternative: Omit initial value and rely on first array element.
nodes.tradeoff: Safer for empty arrays, with a tiny bit of extra syntax.

nodes.title: Use one reduce pass for everything
nodes.why: It validates and sums in one traversal, avoiding extra array allocations.
nodes.tradeoff: Efficient and compact, but packs more logic into one callback.

nodes.title: Guard each element before adding
nodes.why: The guard ensures only valid numeric values affect the running total.
nodes.tradeoff: No intermediate list, but reducer body becomes more verbose.

nodes.title: Always return the accumulator
nodes.why: Reducers must return the next accumulator each iteration to keep state flowing.
nodes.alternative: Mutate external state outside reduce (not recommended).
nodes.tradeoff: Correct reducer contract, though easy to forget when writing quickly.
